channel.title: attacks on Dr. Soumyo Maity
channel.description: Recent content in attacks on Dr. Soumyo Maity

item.title: Zero-Day Attacks
item.description: Zero-day attacks are a type of cyber attack that exploits a vulnerability in software that is unknown to the software vendor. This means that there is no security patch available to fix the vulnerability, and attackers can exploit it to gain access to systems or steal data. Zero-day attacks are a serious threat to businesses and individuals. They can be used to steal sensitive data, such as credit card numbers or passwords, or to disrupt critical systems.

item.title: Denial-of-service (DoS) attacks: A threat to your website
item.description: A denial-of-service (DoS) attack is an attempt to make a website or server unavailable to its intended users. DoS attacks achieve this by flooding the target with so much traffic that it is unable to handle legitimate requests. There are two main types of DoS attacks: Volume-based DoS attacks: These attacks send a large volume of traffic to the target, overwhelming its resources. Protocol-based DoS attacks: These attacks exploit vulnerabilities in the target’s protocols, such as HTTP or TCP.

item.title: Side-Channel Attacks: Sneaking a peek at secrets
item.description: Side-channel attacks are a type of attack that exploits information that is leaked by a cryptographic system during its normal operation. This information can be leaked in a variety of ways, such as through power consumption, timing, or electromagnetic emissions. Side-channel attacks can be used to steal secrets, such as passwords, encryption keys, or other sensitive data. They can also be used to bypass security controls, such as firewalls and intrusion detection systems.
